title: Échange Piliers
sources: README.md: Community alignment and consensus-building objectives
quests.ts: Foundational quest specifications for District E
database.sql: Memo archival and social role tracking
forom-config.ts: Category E (Piliers) and Action 1 (Échange) labels
App.tsx: Quest reward (2 PX) and internal economy redistribution
resume:
The quest 'Échange Piliers' (E1) represents the first collaborative refinement of the community's thematic foundations within the 10x10 Finite Rom Map grid. Located at the intersection of District E (Piliers) and the 'Échange' action (1), this quest is the essential step of opening the floor for dialogue, debate, and consensus regarding the core values initially sparked in E0. In Phase 1: The Foundations (V1), the mission to 'Save human communities and long-term memory' requires more than just top-down ideation; it necessitates a collective agreement on the pillars that will support the node for a decade. This exchange is where the 9 Super-Moderators and early members stress-test the community's ideological DNA, ensuring that the thematic anchors are resilient, inclusive, and aligned with the server’s sovereign goals.

Technically, every contribution to this strategic dialogue is archived in the local PostgreSQL database within the 'memos' table. This ensures that the history of the community’s internal discourse is preserved as sovereign 'Deep Lore,' rather than being fed into the data-mining algorithms of centralized social media platforms. The Forom architecture, built with React 19 and Vite 7, renders these exchanges through a spatial 5x5 viewport, allowing members to see the evolution of their shared values in a high-performance, visual context. This local hosting on hardware like an NVIDIA Jetson is a critical pillar of the 'Minecraft Server' philosophy, where the community owns the physical keys to its own intellectual and moral development.

Economically, completing the E1 quest rewards the contributor with 2 Pixels (PX) and 10 XP. This incentive structure is vital for kickstarting the internal economy and redistributing purchasing power, especially for the 'Social' role (Blue alignment) whose primary function is to facilitate these very networking and consensus-building activities. For pilot projects like the 70 ETS Student Clubs, E1 is the mechanism that ensures club identity is not just dictated by founders but is a shared reality for all members. Every entry at coordinate E1 becomes a permanent part of the V1 Archive, providing the context that ROM, the local RAG engine, will use in Phase 3 to understand the nuance of community alignment. Ultimately, E1 transforms individual ideas into a collective mandate, securing the node’s cultural integrity for the decade ahead. This is the path to true digital sovereignty. Our memory is our most precious asset. Yes.
summary: This quest facilitates the collective exchange and refinement of core thematic pillars at coordinate E1, ensuring community alignment and the preservation of sovereign discourse.
